assurance that brings comfort and inspires effort: "Cast thy bread upon the waters: for thou shalt find it after many days" (Ensign, May 1993, 50).

To the conductor, for the sons of Korah. Hear this, all you peoples. As it is written (Ecclesiastes 11:7), "Truly the light is sweet, and a pleasant thing it is for the eyes to behold the sun." Rabbi Yitzchak and Rabbi Abba bar Kahana said: Rabbi Yitzchak said, "The light of the world to come is sweet." Happy is he who has good deeds to his credit, so that he may behold the light. As it is said…

CHAPTER XI. Charity—Death—Day of j Ce thy bread upon the waters ; for thou shalt find it after many days.:

This challenge affects me as a priest and all of us as Aaronic Priesthood bearers. This is a moral challenge and responsibility, and through our advancement in the priesthood we can learn to follow the right road and the right path to eternal salvation. Our responsibility as young men of this Church to keep ourselves on the right path and to gain eternal salvation is as important today as it was…
